changesProposed.name: AST SpaceMobile

verified.name: AST SpaceMobile
verified.description: Publicly traded satellite manufacturer building the SpaceMobile constellation, a space-based cellular broadband network designed to connect directly to standard smartphones with 4G and 5G coverage globally.
verified.notes: Company founded in May 2017 as AST & Science LLC; went public via SPAC merger with New Providence in April 2021 (Nasdaq: ASTS). Wikipedia notes 578 employees as of 2024. As of Q2 2025, company has raised over $2 billion total.

changesProposed.name: Cerebras

current.name: Cerebras
current.founder: Andrew Feldman, Gary Lauterbach, Michael James, Sean Lie, Jean-Philippe Fricker

verified.name: Cerebras Systems Inc.
verified.description: Cerebras designs and manufactures wafer-scale AI chips and systems for artificial intelligence training and inference workloads. The company's products include the Wafer-Scale Engine (WSE) processor and CS-series AI computer systems.
verified.notes: Company is now trading on Nasdaq (ticker: CBRS) as stated in source 0. Wikipedia lists 2025 revenue of $510 million and 2025 net income of $87.9 million (source 2). Sources reference IPO and pricing around $185 per share raising ~$5.55 billion (source 5), but specific total_raised and valuation figures cannot be definitively verified from these sources due to formatting issues and lack of clear completion in source text.

changesProposed.name: Nuro

verified.name: Nuro
verified.description: Autonomous vehicle technology company that initially developed custom autonomous delivery vehicles and has since pivoted to licensing its Level 4 driving system, Nuro Driver, to automakers and mobility providers.
verified.notes: Company pivoted from autonomous delivery vehicles to licensing autonomy technology in September 2024. Series E funding round opened in April 2025 for $106M and closed in August 2025 with additional $97M. In August 2025, raised $203M at $6B valuation. Partnership with Uber and Lucid Motors for robotaxi deployment of at least 20,000 vehicles over six years announced July 2025.

changesProposed.name: OpenAI

current.name: OpenAI
current.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

verified.name: OpenAI
verified.description: American artificial intelligence research organization that developed the GPT family of large language models, DALL-E series of text-to-image models, and Sora series of text-to-video models. OpenAI released ChatGPT in November 2022, which catalyzed widespread interest in generative AI.
verified.notes: Valuation updated to $500B based on October 2025 share sale ($6.6B at $500B valuation). Company transitioned from nonprofit (2015) to capped for-profit (2019) to for-profit PBC (2025). Microsoft invested over $13B total.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and artificial intelligence company that develops rockets, spacecraft, and satellite constellation technology. As of 2026, SpaceX conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states 'SpaceX is not publicly traded but is expected to have an initial public offering (IPO) in 2026.' Headquarters location changed from El Segundo, California (2002) to Starbase, Texas. Source [0] is a domain sale page and is not usable. Sources [2], [3], [4] are news articles with insufficient company-level information for verification.
